title: dryopteris chaerophylloides var. sericea
intro: Dryopteris chaerophylloides var. sericea is a variety of the Dryopteris chaerophylloides fern species, commonly known as the silverback fern, and is characterized by its silvery underside. This plant is used in traditional medicine, particularly in East Asia, and is valued for its potential medicinal properties, which include anti-inflammatory and antiseptic effects. The health benefits of Dryopteris chaerophylloides var. sericea are believed to include reducing fever, relieving pain and inflammation, and acting as an antimicrobial agent. In herbal preparations, the plant's leaves are typically dried and made into teas, infusions, or decoctions, which are used to harness its medicinal properties.
